name: Izuto Kutsu

appearance.build: Athletic, solid. The engine protrusions on his legs are always visible — mechanical components flush with his calves, permanently present, not painful.
appearance.other: When Engine activates the protrusions heat up and a faint exhaust-heat shimmer comes off his legs. At full output there's a low mechanical hum. He can hear it; usually other people can too, at close range.

personality.summary: Intelligent and self-reliant in the mode of someone who figured out early that relying on others was slower. Not cold — engaged, direct, competent. Has a harder time accepting help than he does giving it, which is a consistent failure mode he's aware of and doesn't entirely know how to fix.
personality.in_class: Excellent student. Works independently. Does group projects by doing them and asking others to review the result.
personality.in_combat: Tactical. He uses Engine's speed for positioning and extraction, not just aggression — getting allies out of bad positions, creating flanking opportunities, controlling the map.
personality.quirk_of_character: He maintains his own leg-engine components like someone maintaining a motorcycle — periodic cleaning, specific care routines. Other students found this normal. Then they realised it was his body he was maintaining with a wrench. It's still normal. It's just also a bit strange.

ability_scores.note: DEX primary — Engine is a speed/mobility mutation, DEX governs both the movement mechanics and the precision of high-speed maneuvering. INT secondary for tactical positioning use and self-maintenance knowledge.

class_note: Heroic Vanguard → Speedster Style. Engine's leg-based movement multiplier and exhaust-burst attacks are the Speedster's core mechanical loop.

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Engine burns through the body's natural fluids, which can only be replenished with juice — a fully hydrated person carries about 32 cups of reserve. Below 8 cups, disadvantage on CON saves and damage dice drop one step; at 0 cups the Quirk shuts down entirely and Izuto gains a level of exhaustion until he drinks juice again.

abilities.name: Engines
abilities.description: Base speed becomes 60 ft, vertical jump 30 ft. The leg-engines work as a natural weapon: on a hit, 1d10 + DEX modifier bludgeoning damage, doubled against structures. Izuto never provokes opportunity attacks and gains +3 to initiative.

abilities.name: Recipro Burst
abilities.type: Bonus Action (3rd level, uses = proficiency bonus, costs 5 cups of juice)
abilities.description: Forcibly accelerate the Engine for 10 seconds: double speed and advantage on attacks for 5 rounds. Attacks push the target back 10 ft and damage die becomes a d12. Afterward, speed drops to 0 and he can't move for 5 rounds, or until cooled by water or ice.

abilities.name: Recipro Extend
abilities.type: Bonus Action (7th level, uses = half proficiency bonus, costs 8 cups of juice)
abilities.description: An extended Recipro Burst: double speed and attack advantage with one extra damage die for 8 rounds, pushing targets back 20 ft. Afterward, speed drops to 0 and he can't move for 8 rounds, or until cooled by water or ice.

player_notes.role_summary: The class's mobility engine — in the most literal sense. You control where the fight happens. Engines gives you raw speed and a natural-weapon kick at baseline; Recipro Burst (and later Recipro Extend) are your big speed-and-damage windows, but mind the juice cost and the forced stop afterward.
player_notes.social_position: Quietly central. He doesn't take leadership but he takes responsibility, which ends up looking similar.
player_notes.growth_hook: He doesn't accept help. This will cost him something at some point. The character moment is asking — sincerely, with nothing held back.
player_notes.suggested_background: Grew up in a family that prized self-sufficiency. Older relatives in trade work. He learned to fix things and assume he was the one who fixed them.

dm_notes.combat_role: Mobile striker and team repositioner. His Engine speed and Recipro Burst windows are some of the highest-value mobility tools in the class. Don't let him solo — his best play is creating advantages for others.
dm_notes.social_role: The self-sufficient one. Reliable. Somewhat isolated by choice. The class respects this and also, quietly, wants in.
dm_notes.growth_arc: Letting people in without framing it as weakness. Someone has to get far enough inside the defences that he doesn't realise they're there until they already are.
dm_notes.weakness_to_exploit: Engine strain in extended high-gear fights. Cold environments reduce max output. His CHA is his weakest social stat — in political or persuasion situations he's at a disadvantage. Self-reliance can become a liability when the team needs him to communicate what he needs.
